whip.title: The Whip — Subject: Rita K.
whip.text: She found the shop on Oranienstraße. She was not looking. The whip chose her the moment she crossed the threshold. Three days later she could no longer remember her own surname. The shopkeeper noted: "Faster than usual."

wig.title: The Wig — Subject: Unknown
wig.text: The auburn wig has been in the cabinet since 1887. Every owner dies with a different face. The last one was found in a hotel in Vienna, smiling. She had been dead for three days. The wig was back on its shelf the next morning.

hourglass.title: The Hourglass — Warning
hourglass.text: DO NOT FLIP IT. The sand flows upward for a reason. The last person who turned it did not age — they un-aged. They are now seven years old and do not recognize their children. The shopkeeper refuses to discuss it.

tarot.title: The Tarot Box — The Strength Card
tarot.text: The deck has 79 cards. One more than standard. The extra card has no name, only a drawing of a door. Those who draw it disappear for exactly one lunar cycle. They always return. They never speak of where they went.

mirror.title: The Mirror — Observation
mirror.text: It does not show your reflection. It shows who you will become. A young woman looked into it and saw herself old, surrounded by books. She became a librarian. A soldier looked into it and saw nothing. He died that winter.

keeper.title: The Shopkeeper — Note to Self
keeper.text: He has no name. He claims he never had one. The shop existed before the street. The street existed before the city. He accepts any coin but not money. "There is a difference," he says. No one has ever seen him eat, sleep, or leave.

compass.title: The Compass — Subject: Elias M.
compass.text: The compass does not point north. It points to where you need to be. Elias followed it for forty days. It led him across three countries, through a forest, into a cave. Inside the cave: a letter from his dead mother, written the day before she died. It said: "Stop looking."

entity.title: The Entity Behind the Shop
entity.text: Some files reference "the Maker." Not the shopkeeper — something older. The artifacts are not cursed. They are tests. Those who pass them are remembered. Those who fail are forgotten. The shop keeps score. The walls of this room are the scoreboard.

map.title: Map of Berlin — Kreuzberg
map.text:
Red threads connect seven points across the city. Oranienstraße. Görlitzer Park. The canal. The old post office on Wiener Straße. A cellar bar with no sign. A courtyard that doesn't appear on Google Maps. And the shop itself — unmarked, unnumbered, but always there if you know where not to look.

The map is old. The threads are new. Someone is still tracking something.
map.source: Pinned to the wall — updated weekly

journal.title: The Journal
journal.text: The journal lies open. The last entry is in a handwriting you almost recognize.

author.title: A Sealed Letter — Opened Once
author.text:
Behind every nameless shop stands someone with a name.

Mary-Ann Black writes stories about objects that remember more than people do. Every artifact in this shop carries a curse, a memory, or a promise. Sometimes all three.

The Namenlos universe is not a series — it is a world. One shop. Different artifacts. Different genres. Different doors to walk through.

The first door is already open.
author.source: Found tucked behind the map — unsigned
